title: Atoll Configuration
description: Configuration file for Atoll, a .NET-native framework inspired by Astro. Place this file as 'atoll.json' in your project root.

$schema.description: JSON Schema reference for IntelliSense support.

site.description: The site URL for generating canonical URLs and sitemap entries (e.g., 'https://example.com'). Defaults to empty string.

base.description: The base path prefix for the site (e.g., '/docs'). All generated URLs are relative to this path. Must start with '/'.

outDir.description: The output directory for the built site, relative to the project root.

srcDir.description: The source directory containing page components, relative to the project root.

publicDir.description: The public directory containing static assets to copy as-is to the output directory root.

server.description: Server-specific configuration for the 'atoll dev' command.

port.description: The port number to listen on.

build.description: Build-specific configuration for the 'atoll build' command.

minify.description: Whether to minify CSS and JS output.

fingerprint.description: Whether to fingerprint asset filenames for cache busting.

clean.description: Whether to clean the output directory before building.

concurrency.description: The maximum number of pages to render in parallel. A value of -1 uses the system default.
